next_steps: Run enrich_iocs_tool with extracted IOCs to get reputation data (or re-run run_pipeline with enrich=True).
Run kql_for_technique / spl_for_technique / eql_for_technique for SIEM-native detection rules for: T1005, T1071.001, T1555.001, T1548.006, T1059.002.
Run yara_for_technique for file-based YARA scanning rules.
Deploy Sigma rules to your SIEM using sigmac or pySigma. Pair with Security-Detections-MCP (list_by_mitre) to check existing coverage.

iocs.notes: Context-aware extraction. Threshold: 0.35. Filtered 2 candidates (RFC1918, bundles, benign, low-confidence). Verify all IOCs before blocking.

enrichment.skipped: Set enrich=True to query VirusTotal / OTX / AbuseIPDB / URLhaus.

techniques.note: 9 technique(s) suppressed (confidence < 0.45). Lower confidence_threshold or check suppressed[] to see them.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Beaconing pattern — periodic HTTP/S requests to uncommon domains at regular intervals.

queries.splunk: index=proxy | stats count, dc(url) as unique_urls, range(_time) as duration by src_ip, dest_host | where count > 20 AND unique_urls < 3 AND duration > 3600 | sort -count
queries.kql: CommonSecurityLog | where DeviceVendor == "Zscaler" or DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks" | summarize RequestCount=count(), UniqueURLs=dcount(RequestURL), Duration=datetime_diff("second", max(TimeGenerated), min(TimeGenerated)) by SourceIP, DestinationHostName | where RequestCount > 20 and UniqueURLs < 3 and Duration > 3600

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-volume DNS queries to newly registered or low-reputation domains from workstations.

queries.splunk: index=dns query_type=A | stats count by src_ip, query | where count > 50 | lookup threat_intel_domains query OUTPUT reputation | where reputation="malicious"
queries.kql: DnsEvents | where QueryType == "A" | summarize count() by ClientIP, Name | where count_ > 50

hypotheses.hypothesis: sqlite3 writing to TCC.db to grant permissions — bypasses macOS privacy controls without user prompt.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr process_name=sqlite3 target_path="*TCC.db*" | table _time, host, user, cmdline, parent_process
queries.kql: DeviceFileEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FileName == "TCC.db" | where InitiatingProcessFileName == "sqlite3" | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine

hypotheses.log_source: EDR on macOS — TCC parent-child chain
hypotheses.hypothesis: Finder or SystemUIServer directed by an untrusted process to rename or move the com.apple.TCC directory.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr process_name IN ("Finder","SystemUIServer") | where NOT match(parent_process_name,"(?i)(loginwindow|launchd)") | table _time, host, user, parent_process_name, cmdline
queries.kql: DeviceProcessEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FileName in~ ("Finder","SystemUIServer") | where not InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("loginwindow","launchd") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
queries.elastic: process.name:(Finder OR SystemUIServer) AND NOT process.parent.name:(loginwindow OR launchd)

hypotheses.hypothesis: osascript invoked with -e flag or reading from stdin — common pattern for in-memory AppleScript execution.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr process_name=osascript | search cmdline="-e *" OR cmdline="*curl*" OR cmdline="*http*" | table _time, host, user, cmdline, parent_process
queries.kql: DeviceProcessEvents | where DeviceName has "macOS" or OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FileName == "osascript" | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-e","curl","http","downloadString") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
queries.elastic: process.name:osascript AND (process.command_line:*-e* OR process.command_line:*curl* OR process.command_line:*http*)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Script Editor or osascript spawning curl, python, or bash — multi-stage download chain.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr (parent_process_name="Script Editor" OR parent_process_name="osascript") child_process_name IN ("curl","python","python3","bash","sh") | table _time, host, user, parent_process_name, cmdline
queries.kql: DeviceProcessEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("osascript","Script Editor") | where FileName in~ ("curl","python","python3","bash","sh") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine
queries.elastic: process.parent.name:(osascript OR Script Editor) AND process.name:(curl OR python OR python3 OR bash OR sh)

hypotheses.technique_name: Exfiltration via Web Service (Telegram Bot API)
hypotheses.hypothesis: Large HTTPS POST to api.telegram.org from a non-Telegram process — Telegram Bot API used as exfil channel.

queries.splunk: index=proxy dest_host="api.telegram.org" cs_method=POST | where bytes_out > 50000 | stats sum(bytes_out) as total_bytes, count by src_ip, user_agent | where NOT match(user_agent,"(?i)(TelegramDesktop|Telegram for)")
queries.kql: CommonSecurityLog | where DestinationHostName =~ "api.telegram.org" and RequestMethod == "POST" and SentBytes > 50000 | where not UserAgent has_any ("TelegramDesktop","Telegram for") | summarize TotalBytes=sum(SentBytes), Requests=count() by SourceIP, UserAgent

hypotheses.technique_name: Exfiltration via Web Service (Telegram Bot API)
hypotheses.log_source: EDR on macOS — curl to Telegram
hypotheses.hypothesis: curl or python making POST requests to api.telegram.org with sendDocument or sendMessage endpoints — data exfil via bot.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr process_name IN ("curl","python3","python") cmdline IN ("*api.telegram.org*","*sendDocument*","*sendMessage*") | table _time, host, user, cmdline
queries.kql: DeviceProcessEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FileName in~ ("curl","python3","python") | where ProcessCommandLine has "api.telegram.org" | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine
queries.elastic: process.name:(curl OR python3 OR python) AND process.command_line:*api.telegram.org*

hypotheses.hypothesis: New .plist file written to /Library/LaunchDaemons/ or ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ pointing to a non-Apple binary.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr event_type=file_create target_path IN ("/Library/LaunchDaemons/*","/Library/LaunchAgents/*","*/Library/LaunchAgents/*") | where NOT match(target_path,"(?i)(apple|microsoft|google/chrome|dropbox)") | table _time, host, user, target_path, process_name
queries.kql: DeviceFileEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FolderPath has_any ("/Library/LaunchDaemons/","/Library/LaunchAgents/") | where not FolderPath has_any ("Apple","Microsoft","Dropbox","Google/Chrome") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName
queries.elastic: file.path:(*LaunchDaemons* OR *LaunchAgents*) AND NOT file.path:(*Apple* OR *Microsoft*) AND event.action:creation

hypotheses.hypothesis: launchctl used to load a new daemon from an unusual path — persistence installation.

queries.splunk: index=macos_edr process_name=launchctl cmdline="*load*" | where NOT match(cmdline,"(?i)(apple|system/library|/usr/libexec)") | table _time, host, user, cmdline, parent_process
queries.kql: DeviceProcessEvents | where OSPlatform == "macOS" | where FileName == "launchctl" | where ProcessCommandLine has "load" | where not ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/System/Library","/usr/libexec","Apple") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
queries.elastic: process.name:launchctl AND process.command_line:*load* AND NOT process.command_line:*/System/Library/*

hunt_hypotheses.note: Techniques not in playbook can be enriched via mitre-attack-mcp (get_datacomponents_detecting_technique, get_procedure_examples_by_technique).

rules.note: No production Sigma rule authored for T1005. Use the fallback links to find community rules, or call generate_sigma() with a specific behavior description.

rules.rule_yaml:
title: Suspicious Outbound Connection from Uncommon Process
id: e8655cff-0ee2-5229-888e-c260ed0a17c3
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious outbound connections from processes that typically
  don't make network connections
author: Threat Research MCP
date: '2026-04-27'
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
    - \powershell.exe
    - \cmd.exe
    - \wscript.exe
    - \cscript.exe
    - \mshta.exe
    - \regsvr32.exe
    - \rundll32.exe
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter:
    DestinationIp|startswith:
    - '10.'
    - 192.168.
    - 172.16.
    - 172.17.
    - 172.18.
    - 172.19.
    - 172.20.
    - 172.21.
    - 172.22.
    - 172.23.
    - 172.24.
    - 172.25.
    - 172.26.
    - 172.27.
    - 172.28.
    - 172.29.
    - 172.30.
    - 172.31.
  condition: selection and not filter
level: medium
tags:
- attack.command_and_control
- attack.t1071.001
falsepositives:
- Legitimate administrative scripts
- Software update mechanisms
references:
- https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1071/001/

rule.title: Suspicious Outbound Connection from Uncommon Process
rule.description: Detects suspicious outbound connections from processes that typically don't make network connections
rule.author: Threat Research MCP
